channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Justice Department declined to prosecute Texas AG Paxton in final weeks of Biden's term: AP sources
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department quietly decided in the final weeks of the Biden administration not to prosecute Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, effectively ending the corruption investigation that cast a long shadow over the political career of a close ally of President Donald Trump, The
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The US is negotiating a minerals deal with conflict-hit Congo, a Trump official says
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — A Trump administration official said Thursday the United States is in talks with conflict-plagued Congo on developing its mineral resources under a deal the Congolese president has said could help make his country safer. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about the Trump tariffs upending global trade and markets
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The trade war President Donald Trump promised has begun, threatening the world economy and straining the United States’ longstanding alliances in Europe and Asia.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: CDC staff working on childhood lead exposure, cancer clusters fired
item.description: The permanent staff of the Division of Environmental Health Science and Practice was cut, according to one source.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Snatched pets and livestock deaths blamed on wolves prompt emergency in rural New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Commissioners in a rural New Mexico county say pets are being snatched from front yards and livestock are being maimed and killed by endangered Mexican gray wolves that seem to have no fear of humans, prompting them to declare a state of emergency.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats call for confirmation hearing for Trump DC prosecutor nominee
item.description: Senate Judiciary Democrats are pushing for a rare hearing on a U.S. attorney nominee as they argue President Trump’s pick to lead the office in D.C., Ed Martin, is especially alarming and deserving of further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: A historic Black church took the Proud Boys to court. Now it controls their trademark
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There is so much history between the walls of Metropolitan African Methodist Episcopal Church, which has hosted funerals for Rosa Parks and Frederick Douglass and opened its pews to American presidents and civil rights icons.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ACLU sues Trump administration over NIH grant cancellations
item.description: The lawsuit, filed in Massachusetts district court, said there was no “scientifically-valid explanation or cause” for NIH to cancel the research projects.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5

item.title: Montana's anti-transgender bathroom restrictions are on hold under a judge's order
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked a Montana law that restricts transgender people's use of bathrooms in public buildings. The measure, which Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Air traffic controllers to get more support after a fight and latest near miss at Washington airport
item.description: Air traffic controllers at Washington's Ronald Reagan National Airport will be offered crisis counseling and additional supervision after a fight in the tower and another alarming near miss two months after a midair collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs: Here's how much countries are getting hit
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday announced he was imposing reciprocal tariffs on dozens of nations, citing what he called years of unfair trade practices.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: District attorney lashes out at ICE for detaining suspect in Boston during a trial
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A district attorney in Boston harshly criticized the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on Wednesday after one of its agents detained a suspect while he was on trial. ICE agent Brian Sullivan took Wilson Martell-Lebron, 49, into custody last week as he was leaving court.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gravedigger arrested after baby's corpse found on West Virginia cemetery's grounds
item.description: A gravedigger at a Cabell County cemetery was arrested on Wednesday after a baby's corpse from 1982 and a busted casket were found at the Baylous Cemetery last week.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Taylor, ABC 4

item.title: New statue on the 'Five Wise Virgins' at Temple Square
item.description: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints and The Relief Society General Presidency have placed a new artwork on the east side of Temple Square that celebrates women.
item.dc:creator: Renisha Mall, ABC 4

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at DOGE
item.description: President Trump has said that DOGE will end at some point and Musk will return to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Diddy civil suit thrown out after accuser refuses to identify herself
item.description: An accuser's lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs was thrown out this week after "Jane Doe" refused to disclose her identity.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KRON4

item.title: Final report details investigation into deadly 2023 Covenant School shooting
item.description: The report touches on the shooter's planning as well as the details unveiled during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at Trump's DOGE
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — DOGE’s days appear to be numbered. Elon Musk recently suggested that he will be done with his work in the near future. President Donald Trump told reporters this week that “at some point, he’s going to be going back” to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump is set to announce new tariffs as he takes a political and financial gamble
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After weeks of White House hype and public anxiety, President Donald Trump is set on Wednesday to announce a barrage of what he calls reciprocal tariffs on friend and foe alike. The new tariffs, coming on what Trump has named “Liberation Day,” are a bid to boost U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KRON4

item.title: Nintendo announces Switch 2 release date, new features in latest Direct showcase
item.description: After years of waiting, Nintendo fans finally have a release date for the Japanese company's new gaming console.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rules in favor of FDA in dispute over flavored vapes
item.description: A unanimous Supreme Court overturned a lower court ruling and found the FDA acted lawfully when it blocked two vaping companies from marketing fruity and dessert-flavored liquids for their electronic nicotine products.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs plan: What we know - and don't
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday is set to unveil reciprocal tariffs on several nations during a White House event. He has dubbed April 2 as "Liberation Day" in honor of these tariffs, with the ceremony being promoted as an effort to "make America wealthy again.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KTLA 5

item.title: Chocolate bars recalled over possible small stones
item.description: Dutch chocolate maker Tony's Chocoloney has issued a nationwide recall of two of its chocolate products, which may contain small stones.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge holds ICE agent in contempt after he detained suspect during a trial
item.description: ICE agent Brian Sullivan detained Wilson Martell-Lebron last week as he was leaving court. But a Boston Municipal Court judge issued a ruling Monday against Sullivan, arguing that he had deprived Martell-Lebron of his rights.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WGN 9

item.title: Why Tesla may avoid the blow of Trump's auto tariffs
item.description: Elon Musk’s focus on American-made products at Tesla could shield his company from the brunt of President Donald Trump’s new tariffs on foreign vehicles and automobile parts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: Volcano erupts in southwestern Iceland after a nearby town, spa evacuated
item.description: A volcano in southwestern Iceland that has erupted repeatedly for more than a year again belched lava and smoke into the air on Tuesday, just hours after authorities evacuated the few remaining residents of a nearby fishing village.
item.dc:creator: MARCO Di MARCO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man called 'Scamdaddy' sentenced for defrauding Utah-based credit unions out of $450K
item.description: A man who called himself "Scamdaddy" was sentenced for his involvement in a scheme to defraud Utah credit unions out of more than $450,000.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Glendale man among 4 soldiers killed on training mission in Lithuania
item.description: A soldier from Los Angeles County is among the four killed by a training exercise in Lithuania last month. The four soldiers were reported missing last week when their armored vehicle sank in a bog, the New York Times reports. "The four U.S.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5
